main.description: Pre-run configuration chosen before play begins, for use in a play record's `state` array. Cardinality: singleton — at most one entry of this $type per state array; writers replace the entry wholesale. Items applied at the start of a run (eg. meta unlocks from previous runs) should be `games.gamesgamesgamesgames.state.acquisition` entries with `via` set to `starting`. Game-specific values not covered here should be `games.gamesgamesgamesgames.state.setting` entries.

mode.description: For games with different modes of play (eg. a daily challenge), the stable name of the mode of play.

seed.description: The play-through's seed, if it's using seeded play. Serialised as a string to preserve precision for ulong/large-integer seeds.

character.description: For games where a character is choosable, the stable identifier of who the player chose for this run.

difficulty.description: For games where a difficulty level is choosable. If the difficulty isn't easily mappable to an integer that gets higher with harder difficulty, ignore this field and use a modifier or a `state.setting` entry instead.

modifiers.description: Run-wide modifiers active from the start (eg. ascension effects, daily mutators, curses).

items.description: Game-specific lexicons that can hold additional setup information.

modifier.description: A single run-wide modifier active from the start of a play-through.

id.description: Stable, game-defined identifier for this modifier. Language-independent and consistent across uses & game versions.

name.description: Human-readable display name in the locale active at write time. Optional, but recommended so renderers without a localised mapping of `id` can fall back to displaying this.

value.description: Optional magnitude or parameter for this modifier (eg. an ascension level), serialised as a string.
